verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचभृग्वादयस्ते मुनयो लोकानां क्षेमदर्शिन: ।गोप्तर्यसति वै नृणां पश्यन्त: पशुसाम्यताम् ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācabhṛgv-ādayas te munayolokānāṁ kṣema-darśinaḥgoptary asati vai nṝṇāṁpaśyantaḥ paśu-sāmyatām
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: O great hero Vidura, the great sages, headed by Bhṛgu, were always thinking of the welfare of the people in general. When they saw that in the absence of King Aṅga there was no one to protect the interests of the people, they understood that without a ruler the people would become independent and nonregulated.

verses.sanskrit: वीरमातरमाहूय सुनीथां ब्रह्मवादिन: ।प्रकृत्यसम्मतं वेनमभ्यषिञ्चन् पतिं भुव: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: vīra-mātaram āhūyasunīthāṁ brahma-vādinaḥprakṛty-asammataṁ venamabhyaṣiñcan patiṁ bhuvaḥ
verses.text: The great sages then called for the Queen Mother, Sunīthā, and with her permission they installed Vena on the throne as master of the world. All the ministers, however, disagreed with this.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा नृपासनगतं वेनमत्युग्रशासनम् ।निलिल्युर्दस्यव: सद्य: सर्पत्रस्ता इवाखव: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutvā nṛpāsana-gataṁvenam atyugra-śāsanamnililyur dasyavaḥ sadyaḥsarpa-trastā ivākhavaḥ
verses.text: It was already known that Vena was very severe and cruel; therefore, as soon as all the thieves and rogues in the state heard of his ascendance to the royal throne, they became very much afraid of him. Indeed, they hid themselves here and there as rats hide themselves from snakes.

verses.sanskrit: स आरूढनृपस्थान उन्नद्धोऽष्टविभूतिभि: ।अवमेने महाभागान् स्तब्ध: सम्भावित: स्वत: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ārūḍha-nṛpa-sthānaunnaddho ’ṣṭa-vibhūtibhiḥavamene mahā-bhāgānstabdhaḥ sambhāvitaḥ svataḥ
verses.text: When the King ascended to the throne, he became all-powerful with eight kinds of opulences. Consequently he became too proud. By virtue of his false prestige, he considered himself to be greater than anyone. Thus he began to insult great personalities.

verses.sanskrit: एवं मदान्ध उत्सिक्तो निरङ्कुश इव द्विप: ।पर्यटन् रथमास्थाय कम्पयन्निव रोदसी ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ madāndha utsiktoniraṅkuśa iva dvipaḥparyaṭan ratham āsthāyakampayann iva rodasī
verses.text: When he became overly blind due to his opulences, King Vena mounted a chariot and, like an uncontrolled elephant, began to travel through the kingdom, causing the sky and earth to tremble wherever he went.

verses.sanskrit: न यष्टव्यं न दातव्यं न होतव्यं द्विजा: क्‍वचित् ।इति न्यवारयद्धर्मं भेरीघोषेण सर्वश: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: na yaṣṭavyaṁ na dātavyaṁna hotavyaṁ dvijāḥ kvacititi nyavārayad dharmaṁbherī-ghoṣeṇa sarvaśaḥ
verses.text: All the twice-born [brāhmaṇas] were forbidden henceforward to perform any sacrifice, and they were also forbidden to give charity or offer clarified butter. Thus King Vena sounded kettledrums throughout the countryside. In other words, he stopped all kinds of religious rituals.

verses.sanskrit: वेनस्यावेक्ष्य मुनयो दुर्वृत्तस्य विचेष्टितम् ।विमृश्य लोकव्यसनं कृपयोचु: स्म सत्रिण: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: venasyāvekṣya munayodurvṛttasya viceṣṭitamvimṛśya loka-vyasanaṁkṛpayocuḥ sma satriṇaḥ
verses.text: Therefore all the great sages assembled together and, after observing cruel Vena’s atrocities, concluded that a great danger and catastrophe was approaching the people of the world. Thus out of compassion they began to talk amongst themselves, for they themselves were the performers of the sacrifices.

verses.sanskrit: अहो उभयत: प्राप्तं लोकस्य व्यसनं महत् ।दारुण्युभयतो दीप्ते इव तस्करपालयो: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho ubhayataḥ prāptaṁlokasya vyasanaṁ mahatdāruṇy ubhayato dīpteiva taskara-pālayoḥ
verses.text: When the great sages consulted one another, they saw that the people were in a dangerous position from both directions. When a fire blazes on both ends of a log, the ants in the middle are in a very dangerous situation. Similarly, at that time the people in general were in a dangerous position due to an irresponsible king on one side and thieves and rogues on the other.

verses.sanskrit: अराजकभयादेष कृतो राजातदर्हण: ।ततोऽप्यासीद्भयं त्वद्य कथं स्यात्स्वस्ति देहिनाम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: arājaka-bhayād eṣakṛto rājātad-arhaṇaḥtato ’py āsīd bhayaṁ tv adyakathaṁ syāt svasti dehinām
verses.text: Thinking to save the state from irregularity, the sages began to consider that it was due to a political crisis that they made Vena king although he was not qualified. But alas, now the people were being disturbed by the king himself. Under such circumstances, how could the people be happy?

verses.sanskrit: अहेरिव पय:पोष: पोषकस्याप्यनर्थभृत् ।वेन: प्रकृत्यैव खल: सुनीथागर्भसम्भव: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: aher iva payaḥ-poṣaḥpoṣakasyāpy anartha-bhṛtvenaḥ prakṛtyaiva khalaḥsunīthā-garbha-sambhavaḥ
verses.text: The sages began to think within themselves: Because he was born from the womb of Sunīthā, King Vena is by nature very mischievous. Supporting this mischievous king is exactly like maintaining a snake with milk. Now he has become a source of all difficulties.

verses.sanskrit: निरूपित: प्रजापाल: स जिघांसति वै प्रजा: ।तथापि सान्‍त्वयेमामुं नास्मांस्तत्पातकं स्पृशेत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: nirūpitaḥ prajā-pālaḥsa jighāṁsati vai prajāḥtathāpi sāntvayemāmuṁnāsmāṁs tat-pātakaṁ spṛśet
verses.text: We appointed this Vena king of the state in order to give protection to the citizens, but now he has become the enemy of the citizens. Despite all these discrepancies, we should at once try to pacify him. By doing so, we may not be touched by the sinful results caused by him.

verses.sanskrit: तद्विद्वद्‌भिरसद्‍वृत्तो वेनोऽस्माभि: कृतो नृप: ।सान्त्वितो यदि नो वाचं न ग्रहीष्यत्यधर्मकृत् ।लोकधिक्कारसन्दग्धं दहिष्याम: स्वतेजसा ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad-vidvadbhir asad-vṛttoveno ’smābhiḥ kṛto nṛpaḥsāntvito yadi no vācaṁna grahīṣyaty adharma-kṛtloka-dhikkāra-sandagdhaṁdahiṣyāmaḥ sva-tejasā
verses.text: The saintly sages continued thinking: Of course, we were completely aware of his mischievous nature, yet we nevertheless enthroned Vena. If we cannot persuade King Vena to accept our advice, he will be condemned by the public, and we will join them. Thus by our prowess we shall burn him to ashes.

verses.sanskrit: एवमध्यवसायैनं मुनयो गूढमन्यव: ।उपव्रज्याब्रुवन् वेनं सान्‍त्वयित्वा च सामभि: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: evam adhyavasāyainaṁmunayo gūḍha-manyavaḥupavrajyābruvan venaṁsāntvayitvā ca sāmabhiḥ
verses.text: The great sages, having thus decided, approached King Vena. Concealing their real anger, they pacified him with sweet words and then spoke as follows.

verses.sanskrit: मुनय ऊचु:नृपवर्य निबोधैतद्यत्ते विज्ञापयाम भो: ।आयु:श्रीबलकीर्तीनां तव तात विवर्धनम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: munaya ūcuḥnṛpa-varya nibodhaitadyat te vijñāpayāma bhoḥāyuḥ-śrī-bala-kīrtīnāṁtava tāta vivardhanam
verses.text: The great sages said: Dear King, we have come to give you good advice. Kindly hear us with great attention. By doing so, your duration of life and your opulence, strength and reputation will increase.

verses.sanskrit: धर्म आचरित: पुंसां वाङ्‍मन:कायबुद्धिभि: ।लोकान् विशोकान् वतरत्यथानन्त्यमसङ्गिनाम् ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharma ācaritaḥ puṁsāṁvāṅ-manaḥ-kāya-buddhibhiḥlokān viśokān vitaratyathānantyam asaṅginām
verses.text: Those who live according to religious principles and who follow them by words, mind, body and intelligence are elevated to the heavenly kingdom, which is devoid of all miseries. Being thus rid of the material influence, they achieve unlimited happiness in life.

verses.sanskrit: स ते मा विनशेद्वीर प्रजानां क्षेमलक्षण: ।यस्मिन् विनष्टे नृपतिरैश्वर्यादवरोहति ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa te mā vinaśed vīraprajānāṁ kṣema-lakṣaṇaḥyasmin vinaṣṭe nṛpatiraiśvaryād avarohati
verses.text: The sages continued: O great hero, for this reason you should not be the cause of spoiling the spiritual life of the general populace. If their spiritual life is spoiled because of your activities, you will certainly fall down from your opulent and royal position.

verses.sanskrit: राजन्नसाध्वमात्येभ्यश्चोरादिभ्य: प्रजा नृप: ।रक्षन्यथा बलिं गृह्णन्निह प्रेत्य च मोदते ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājann asādhv-amātyebhyaścorādibhyaḥ prajā nṛpaḥrakṣan yathā baliṁ gṛhṇanniha pretya ca modate
verses.text: The saintly persons continued: When the king protects the citizens from the disturbances of mischievous ministers as well as from thieves and rogues, he can, by virtue of such pious activities, accept taxes given by his subjects. Thus a pious king can certainly enjoy himself in this world as well as in the life after death.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य राष्ट्रे पुरे चैव भगवान् यज्ञपूरुष: ।इज्यते स्वेन धर्मेण जनैर्वर्णाश्रमान्वितै: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yasya rāṣṭre pure caivabhagavān yajña-pūruṣaḥijyate svena dharmeṇajanair varṇāśramānvitaiḥ
verses.text: The king is supposed to be pious in whose state and cities the general populace strictly observes the system of eight social orders of varṇa and āśrama, and where all citizens engage in worshiping the Supreme Personality of Godhead by their particular occupations.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य राज्ञो महाभाग भगवान् भूतभावन: ।परितुष्यति विश्वात्मा तिष्ठतो निजशासने ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya rājño mahā-bhāgabhagavān bhūta-bhāvanaḥparituṣyati viśvātmātiṣṭhato nija-śāsane
verses.text: O noble one, if the king sees that the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the original cause of the cosmic manifestation and the Supersoul within everyone, is worshiped, the Lord will be satisfied.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तुष्टे किमप्राप्यं जगतामीश्वरेश्वरे ।लोका: सपाला ह्येतस्मै हरन्ति बलिमाद‍ृता: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmiṁs tuṣṭe kim aprāpyaṁjagatām īśvareśvarelokāḥ sapālā hy etasmaiharanti balim ādṛtāḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead is worshiped by the great demigods who are controllers of universal affairs. When He is satisfied, nothing is impossible to achieve. For this reason all the demigods, presiding deities of different planets, as well as the inhabitants of their planets, take great pleasure in offering all kinds of paraphernalia for His worship.

verses.sanskrit: तं सर्वलोकामरयज्ञसङ्ग्रहंत्रयीमयं द्रव्यमयं तपोमयम् ।यज्ञैर्विचित्रैर्यजतो भवाय तेराजन् स्वदेशाननुरोद्धुमर्हसि ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ sarva-lokāmara-yajña-saṅgrahaṁtrayīmayaṁ dravyamayaṁ tapomayamyajñair vicitrair yajato bhavāya terājan sva-deśān anuroddhum arhasi
verses.text: Dear King, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, along with the predominating deities, is the enjoyer of the results of all sacrifices in all planets. The Supreme Lord is the sum total of the three Vedas, the owner of everything, and the ultimate goal of all austerity. Therefore your countrymen should engage in performing various sacrifices for your elevation. Indeed, you should always direct them towards the offering of sacrifices.

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञेन युष्मद्विषये द्विजातिभि-र्वितायमानेन सुरा: कला हरे: ।स्विष्टा: सुतुष्टा: प्रदिशन्ति वाञ्छितंतद्धेलनं नार्हसि वीर चेष्टितुम् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yajñena yuṣmad-viṣaye dvijātibhirvitāyamānena surāḥ kalā hareḥsviṣṭāḥ sutuṣṭāḥ pradiśanti vāñchitaṁtad-dhelanaṁ nārhasi vīra ceṣṭitum
verses.text: When all the brāhmaṇas engage in performing sacrifices in your kingdom, all the demigods, who are plenary expansions of the Lord, will be very much satisfied by their activities and will give you your desired result. Therefore, O hero, do not stop the sacrificial performances. If you stop them, you will disrespect the demigods.

verses.sanskrit: वेन उवाचबालिशा बत यूयं वा अधर्मे धर्ममानिन: ।ये वृत्तिदं पतिं हित्वा जारं पतिमुपासते ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: vena uvācabāliśā bata yūyaṁ vāadharme dharma-māninaḥye vṛttidaṁ patiṁ hitvājāraṁ patim upāsate
verses.text: King Vena replied: You are not at all experienced. It is very much regrettable that you are maintaining something which is not religious and are accepting it as religious. Indeed, I think you are giving up your real husband, who maintains you, and are searching after some paramour to worship.

verses.sanskrit: अवजानन्त्यमी मूढा नृपरूपिणमीश्वरम् ।नानुविन्दन्ति ते भद्रमिह लोके परत्र च ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: avajānanty amī mūḍhānṛpa-rūpiṇam īśvaramnānuvindanti te bhadramiha loke paratra ca
verses.text: Those who, out of gross ignorance, do not worship the king, who is actually the Supreme Personality of Godhead, experience happiness neither in this world nor in the world after death.

verses.sanskrit: को यज्ञपुरुषो नाम यत्र वो भक्तिरीद‍ृशी ।भर्तृस्‍नेहविदूराणां यथा जारे कुयोषिताम् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ko yajña-puruṣo nāmayatra vo bhaktir īdṛśībhartṛ-sneha-vidūrāṇāṁyathā jāre kuyoṣitām
verses.text: You are so much devoted to the demigods, but who are they? Indeed, your affection for these demigods is exactly like the affection of an unchaste woman who neglects her married life and gives all attention to her paramour.

verses.sanskrit: विष्णुर्विरिञ्चो गिरिश इन्द्रो वायुर्यमो रवि: ।पर्जन्यो धनद: सोम: क्षितिरग्निरपाम्पति: ॥ २६ ॥एते चान्ये च विबुधा: प्रभवो वरशापयो: ।देहे भवन्ति नृपते: सर्वदेवमयो नृप: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: viṣṇur viriñco giriśaindro vāyur yamo raviḥparjanyo dhanadaḥ somaḥkṣitir agnir apāmpatiḥ
verses.text: Lord Viṣṇu; Lord Brahmā; Lord Śiva; Lord Indra; Vāyu, the master of air; Yama, the superintendent of death; the sun-god; the director of rainfall; Kuvera, the treasurer; the moon-god; the predominating deity of the earth; Agni, the fire-god; Varuṇa, the lord of waters; and all others who are great and competent to bestow benedictions or to curse — all abide in the body of the king. For this reason the king is known as the reservoir of all demigods, who are simply parts and parcels of the king’s body.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मान्मां कर्मभिर्विप्रा यजध्वं गतमत्सरा: ।बलिं च मह्यं हरत मत्तोऽन्य: कोऽग्रभुक्पुमान् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmān māṁ karmabhir viprāyajadhvaṁ gata-matsarāḥbaliṁ ca mahyaṁ haratamatto ’nyaḥ ko ’gra-bhuk pumān
verses.text: King Vena continued: For this reason, O brāhmaṇas, you should abandon your envy of me, and by your ritualistic activities you should worship me and offer me all paraphernalia. If you are intelligent, you should know that there is no personality superior to me who can accept the first oblations of all sacrifices.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचइत्थं विपर्ययमति: पापीयानुत्पथं गत: ।अनुनीयमानस्तद्याच्ञां न चक्रे भ्रष्टमङ्गल: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaitthaṁ viparyaya-matiḥpāpīyān utpathaṁ gataḥanunīyamānas tad-yācñāṁna cakre bhraṣṭa-maṅgalaḥ
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya continued: Thus the King, who became unintelligent due to his sinful life and deviation from the right path, became actually bereft of all good fortune. He could not accept the requests of the great sages, which the sages put before him with great respect, and therefore he was condemned.

verses.sanskrit: इति तेऽसत्कृतास्तेन द्विजा: पण्डितमानिना ।भग्नायां भव्ययाच्ञायां तस्मै विदुर चुक्रुधु: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: iti te ’sat-kṛtās tenadvijāḥ paṇḍita-māninābhagnāyāṁ bhavya-yācñāyāṁtasmai vidura cukrudhuḥ
verses.text: My dear Vidura, all good fortune unto you. The foolish King, who thought himself very learned, thus insulted the great sages, and the sages, being brokenhearted by the King’s words, became very angry at him.

verses.sanskrit: हन्यतां हन्यतामेष पाप: प्रकृतिदारुण: ।जीवञ्जगदसावाशु कुरुते भस्मसाद् ध्रुवम् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: hanyatāṁ hanyatām eṣapāpaḥ prakṛti-dāruṇaḥjīvañ jagad asāv āśukurute bhasmasād dhruvam
verses.text: All the great saintly sages immediately cried: Kill him! Kill him! He is the most dreadful, sinful person. If he lives, he will certainly turn the whole world into ashes in no time.

verses.sanskrit: नायमर्हत्यसद्‍वृत्तो नरदेववरासनम् ।योऽधियज्ञपतिं विष्णुं विनिन्दत्यनपत्रप: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāyam arhaty asad-vṛttonaradeva-varāsanamyo ’dhiyajña-patiṁ viṣṇuṁvinindaty anapatrapaḥ
verses.text: The saintly sages continued: This impious, impudent man does not deserve to sit on the throne at all. He is so shameless that he even dared insult the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Viṣṇu.

verses.sanskrit: को वैनं परिचक्षीत वेनमेकमृतेऽशुभम् ।प्राप्त ईद‍ृशमैश्वर्यं यदनुग्रहभाजन: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ko vainaṁ paricakṣītavenam ekam ṛte ’śubhamprāpta īdṛśam aiśvaryaṁyad-anugraha-bhājanaḥ
verses.text: But for King Vena, who is simply inauspicious, who would blaspheme the Supreme Personality of Godhead, by whose mercy one is awarded all kinds of fortune and opulence?

verses.sanskrit: इत्थं व्यवसिता हन्तुमृषयो रूढमन्यव: ।निजघ्नुर्हुङ्कृतैर्वेनं हतमच्युतनिन्दया ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: itthaṁ vyavasitā hantumṛṣayo rūḍha-manyavaḥnijaghnur huṅkṛtair venaṁhatam acyuta-nindayā
verses.text: The great sages, thus manifesting their covert anger, immediately decided to kill the King. King Vena was already as good as dead due to his blasphemy against the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Thus without using any weapons, the sages killed King Vena simply by high-sounding words.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषिभि: स्वाश्रमपदं गते पुत्रकलेवरम् ।सुनीथा पालयामास विद्यायोगेन शोचती ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ṛṣibhiḥ svāśrama-padaṁgate putra-kalevaramsunīthā pālayām āsavidyā-yogena śocatī
verses.text: After all the sages returned to their respective hermitages, the mother of King Vena, Sunīthā, became very much aggrieved because of her son’s death. She decided to preserve the dead body of her son by the application of certain ingredients and by chanting mantras [mantra-yogena].

verses.sanskrit: एकदा मुनयस्ते तु सरस्वत्सलिलाप्लुता: ।हुत्वाग्नीन् सत्कथाश्चक्रुरुपविष्टा: सरित्तटे ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: ekadā munayas te tusarasvat-salilāplutāḥhutvāgnīn sat-kathāś cakrurupaviṣṭāḥ sarit-taṭe
verses.text: Once upon a time, the same saintly persons, after taking their bath in the river Sarasvatī, began to perform their daily duties by offering oblations into the sacrificial fires. After this, sitting on the bank of the river, they began to talk about the transcendental person and His pastimes.

verses.sanskrit: वीक्ष्योत्थितांस्तदोत्पातानाहुर्लोकभयङ्करान् ।अप्यभद्रमनाथाया दस्युभ्यो न भवेद्भुव: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: vīkṣyotthitāṁs tadotpātānāhur loka-bhayaṅkarānapy abhadram anāthāyādasyubhyo na bhaved bhuvaḥ
verses.text: In those days there were various disturbances in the country that were creating a panic in society. Therefore all the sages began to talk amongst themselves: Since the King is dead and there is no protector in the world, misfortune may befall the people in general on account of rogues and thieves.

verses.sanskrit: एवं मृशन्त ऋषयो धावतां सर्वतोदिशम् ।पांसु: समुत्थितो भूरिश्चोराणामभिलुम्पताम् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ mṛśanta ṛṣayodhāvatāṁ sarvato-diśampāṁsuḥ samutthito bhūriścorāṇām abhilumpatām
verses.text: When the great sages were carrying on their discussion in this way, they saw a dust storm arising from all directions. This storm was caused by the running of thieves and rogues, who were engaged in plundering the citizens.

verses.sanskrit: तदुपद्रवमाज्ञाय लोकस्य वसु लुम्पताम् ।भर्तर्युपरते तस्मिन्नन्योन्यं च जिघांसताम् ॥ ३९ ॥चोरप्रायं जनपदं हीनसत्त्वमराजकम् ।लोकान्नावारयञ्छक्ता अपि तद्दोषदर्शिन: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: tad upadravam ājñāyalokasya vasu lumpatāmbhartary uparate tasminnanyonyaṁ ca jighāṁ-satām
verses.text: Upon seeing the dust storm, the saintly persons could understand that there were a great deal of irregularities due to the death of King Vena. Without government, the state was devoid of law and order, and consequently there was a great uprising of murderous thieves and rogues, who were plundering the riches of the people in general. Although the great sages could subdue the disturbance by their powers — just as they could kill the King — they considered it improper on their part to do so. Thus they did not attempt to stop the disturbance.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मण: समद‍ृक् शान्तो दीनानां समुपेक्षक: ।स्रवते ब्रह्म तस्यापि भिन्नभाण्डात्पयो यथा ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇaḥ sama-dṛk śāntodīnānāṁ samupekṣakaḥsravate brahma tasyāpibhinna-bhāṇḍāt payo yathā
verses.text: The great sages began to think that although a brāhmaṇa is peaceful and impartial because he is equal to everyone, it is still not his duty to neglect poor humans. By such neglect, a brāhmaṇa’s spiritual power diminishes, just as water kept in a cracked pot leaks out.

verses.sanskrit: नाङ्गस्य वंशो राजर्षेरेष संस्थातुमर्हति ।अमोघवीर्या हि नृपा वंशेऽस्मिन् केशवाश्रया: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāṅgasya vaṁśo rājarṣereṣa saṁsthātum arhatiamogha-vīryā hi nṛpāvaṁśe ’smin keśavāśrayāḥ
verses.text: The sages decided that the descendants of the family of the saintly King Aṅga should not be stopped, for in this family the semen was very powerful and the children were prone to become devotees of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: विनिश्चित्यैवमृषयो विपन्नस्य महीपते: ।ममन्थुरूरुं तरसा तत्रासीद्बाहुको नर: ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: viniścityaivam ṛṣayovipannasya mahīpateḥmamanthur ūruṁ tarasātatrāsīd bāhuko naraḥ
verses.text: After making a decision, the saintly persons and sages churned the thighs of the dead body of King Vena with great force and according to a specific method. As a result of this churning, a dwarflike person was born from King Vena’s body.

verses.sanskrit: काककृष्णोऽतिह्रस्वाङ्गो ह्रस्वबाहुर्महाहनु: ।ह्रस्वपान्निम्ननासाग्रो रक्ताक्षस्ताम्रमूर्धज: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kāka-kṛṣṇo ’tihrasvāṅgohrasva-bāhur mahā-hanuḥhrasva-pān nimna-nāsāgroraktākṣas tāmra-mūrdhajaḥ
verses.text: This person born from King Vena’s thighs was named Bāhuka, and his complexion was as black as a crow’s. All the limbs of his body were very short, his arms and legs were short, and his jaws were large. His nose was flat, his eyes were reddish, and his hair copper-colored.

verses.sanskrit: तं तु तेऽवनतं दीनं किं करोमीति वादिनम् ।निषीदेत्यब्रुवंस्तात स निषादस्ततोऽभवत् ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ tu te ’vanataṁ dīnaṁkiṁ karomīti vādinamniṣīdety abruvaṁs tātasa niṣādas tato ’bhavat
verses.text: He was very submissive and meek, and immediately after his birth he bowed down and inquired, “Sirs, what shall I do?” The great sages replied, “Please sit down [niṣīda].” Thus Niṣāda, the father of the Naiṣāda race, was born.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य वंश्यास्तु नैषादा गिरिकाननगोचरा: ।येनाहरज्जायमानो वेनकल्मषमुल्बणम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya vaṁśyās tu naiṣādāgiri-kānana-gocarāḥyenāharaj jāyamānovena-kalmaṣam ulbaṇam
verses.text: After his [Niṣāda’s] birth, he immediately took charge of all the resultant actions of King Vena’s sinful activities. As such, this Naiṣāda class are always engaged in sinful activities like stealing, plundering and hunting. Consequently they are only allowed to live in the hills and forests.
